rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF VIRGINIA CAROLINE MARTINEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #13, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/17/21. Total loan interest reimbursements were $5,484.00 from the two co-conservators, or $2,742.00 each, but total consumer loan payments disbursed were $11,189.43, 1/3 of which would be $3,729.81. Caretaker payments to each of the co-conservators ceased in March 2020, but it appears that the conservatee was moved to a board and care facility in October 2019 and to another by February 2020. Meals and groceries were paid in even amounts in the hundreds? How much was the total monthly storage facility? The date of any order authorizing fees must be set forth in the disbursements. Unspecified legal costs totaling $118.18 will not be allowed without more. If time was billed by a paralegal, the fee declaration must include a statement addressing his/her qualifications regarding the requirements of, and compliance with, Business & Professions Code Section 6450. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the Examiner before 2/8/21.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALBERT SUM AND ROSE SUM REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum V. Kaiser Permanente Antioch Medical
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. David and Charlene's Motion to Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum V. Kaiser Permanente Antioch Medical is DENIED. Steven, David and Charlene are siblings, the children of Albert and Rose Sum. The surviving spouse, Rose, died February 16, 2020. In June 2020, Steven Sum ("Steven") filed a Petition to Invalidate Trust for Lack of Capacity and Undue Influence; for Financial Elder Abuse; and for Breach of Fiduciary Duty. Steven seeks enforcement of the terms of the Albert Sum and Rose Sum Revocable Trust, dated August 12, 1992. It is worth noting that David is named as executor in Rose's 1992 Will. Two months later, David Sum and Charlene Sum ("David and Charlene") filed a Petition for Elder Abuse Against Steven Sum. David and Charlene seek enforcement of a 2017 Restatement of the Trust. Both petitions raise issues regarding Rose Sum's capacities and health generally in the decade between 2010 and 2020. David served a subpoena duces tecum on Kaiser Permanente Antioch Medical for Rose's medical records from 2010 through 2020. David and Charlene moved to quash the subpoena under Code of Civil Procedure section 1987.1, subdivision (a) ("Section 1987.1(a)") on the grounds that Steven's request "is unlawful and [an] indirect violation of HIPAA laws, specifically 45 CFR [section] 164.52 ["Regulation 164.52"]." The subpoenaed information is relevant to this proceeding or is likely to lead to the discovery of relevant information. (See Evid. Code, sections 1000, 1019.) David and Charlene have failed to carry their burden under Section 1987.1(a) in that they have not shown grounds justifying the quashing of the subpoena, nor have they shown that allowing the production will expose them to "unreasonable or oppressive demands, including unreasonable violations of their right of privacy of the person." (See Code Civ. Proc., section 1987.1, subd. (a).) David and Charlene rely on Regulation 164.52 to argue that they are defending Rose's privacy or interests. They're reliance is misplaced in that they have not shown that they are decedent's executor, administrator, or "or other person [who] has authority to act on behalf of a deceased individual or of the individual's estate." (See 45 CFR section 164.502(g)(4).) It appears that David, who is the executor of the will, is the person who would qualify under Regulation 164.52. In any event, on this record, the court is not persuaded that Charlene and David's status as co-trustees with Steven under the 2017 Restatement of the Trust gives them authority to act on Rose's behalf as to health record disclosures. The court does not reach requests made in the reply brief. Petitioner shall lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, prior to the date of the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on January 19, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN BENJAMIN STECZYNSKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required to admit a lost will, although there are still procedural issues. The proof of service filed on 12/15/20 did not clearly indicate on the face of the notice that an amended petition was filed and will be heard. If the Court does not wish to waive defective notice, the hearing may be continued again. As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 8th amended general order on the courtâs website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS ELKING WESTERBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/8/21. Notice mailed to the paternal half-aunt and -uncles was the wrong form of Notice. The correct Notice of Petition to Administer Estate must be mailed to those three heirs at least 15 days before 2/8/21. Decedent's biological father, Robert Elking, reportedly had one full sibling, Patricia Ann Elking Rowe, who died in 2006. The petitioner's second supplement does not disclose whether or not that paternal aunt was survived by any issue. If she was not survived by any issue, that statement should be filed for the record in a verified third supplement. If she was survived by issue, those cousins must be set forth in a verified third supplement and must be given (or must waive) notice. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the Examiner before 1/29/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCES RUTH HIGGINS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/16/2021. Issues: 1) The First Amendment is not signed or verified by Petitioner. [CRC 7.103] 2) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAUREEN E. CROWE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/8/21. No proof of publication of the amended petition has been filed yet. [CRC 7.53(a).] (Item 3e(1) of the amended petition should have been checked to indicate that will waives bond. A copy of the Connecticut order appointing Patricia Callahan in Edward Callahan's estate is not the same as Letters. After notice issues are addressed, an appearance may be required.) Courtesy copies of the proof of publication or any other papers filed after 12/31/20 must be received by the Examiner by 1/29/21. If notice of the amended petition has not yet been published and there is not sufficient time to publish notice of the amended petition for the 2/8/21 hearing, Counsel should contact the Examiner to request additional tme.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AMALIA ALTAMIRANO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/08/2021 as less than 15-day notice was given to additional persons having legal custody of and residing with minor beneficiaries of devisee trust.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICK W CONLON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: continued to 2/8/21. unfiled documents were delivered to the examiner, and examiners do not file documents. in addition, the amended Duties and Liabilities form must be e-filed, while the confidential attachment to the duties and liabilities form must be submitted with its own caption page, and either dropped off in the drop boxes in the courthouse or mailed (it cannot be efiled)to Room 103 for filing. deadline for completing the tasks is noon on 2/1/21. the examiner kept the proposed order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NICHOLAS M GRAVES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/8/21 at request of counsel.
